summary.intent: Address a fourth round of Codex review comments on PR #681 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically fixing TimeField state preservation during unrelated prop syncs and an ARIA live-region politeness mismatch.
summary.outcome: All three new Codex threads were resolved with targeted regressions and fixes: SyncProps now preserves active typeahead buffer/segment state, the error-message element gained aria-live="polite", and branch-coverage gaps from the aborted mutation run were closed with a narrow unit test. The fourth-pass commit was pushed and a fifth Codex review triggered.

repo: The repo uses cargo-insta for snapshot testing; changing ARIA attributes on rendered output requires an intentional snapshot acceptance step (cargo insta accept) and the snapshot-reviewed label must be reapplied after each push that touches .snap files.
CI pipeline order: Snapshot Change Check → Formatting → Clippy → Workspace Check → Mutual Exclusion Guard → Unit Tests, with each stage gating the next.
Codex signals active review via 👀 reaction and posts findings as GraphQL review threads; the team polls both reactions and unresolved threads to detect new feedback.
cargo mutants --iterate is used for mutation testing of specific files; two missed mutants were found in the must_rebuild condition before the run was killed.
The project uses cargo xtask spec validate for spec conformance and a separate proptest suite (proptest_state_machines) for property-based state machine testing.

code.finding: The must_rebuild condition had two missed mutant branches: forced-width formatting changes and clamped value changes. These were not covered by existing tests and were exposed by the partial mutation run.

SyncProps was clearing the typeahead buffer and rebuilding the in-progress segment from stale published values when unrelated props changed. Fix gates segment rebuilds to structural/value changes only.
Snapshot required update after adding aria-live="polite" to the error-message element; the change was intentional and accepted.

workflow: Red-green loop discipline: write failing regressions first, then implement the fix, then verify regressions pass before committing.
Lighter verification set for review iterations (unit tests + fmt + clippy + spec conformance + coverage) is faster than including mutation testing every cycle.
Mutation testing should only be run once per major change, not on every review-loop iteration, as it is too slow for rapid feedback cycles.
When mutation testing surfaces concrete missed branches before being killed, add narrow targeted unit tests for those branches rather than rerunning the full mutation suite.
cargo +nightly fmt --all --check can fail on newly added test code; apply formatting before the final check rather than writing pre-formatted test bodies.

friction: Mutation testing (cargo mutants --iterate) is prohibitively slow for per-review-cycle use; the user had to explicitly stop the practice mid-session.
Two concrete missed mutants were surfaced just before the mutation run was killed, creating a partial result that still required follow-up work.
The snapshot-reviewed label must be manually reapplied after every push that touches .snap files, creating a recurring toil step.
Repeated 120-second sleep/poll loops to wait for Codex reaction and CI status add significant dead time to the review cycle.
cargo insta accept initially required two invocations (with and without -p flag) before succeeding.
open_items: A fifth Codex review pass was triggered at the end of the session; its findings are unknown and will require another fix iteration.
DateField (date_field/mod.rs) may have the same SyncProps focus-preservation bug as TimeField — the transcript reads both files but only fixes TimeField.
Mutation testing was stopped mid-run with ~170 active mutants; the remaining mutant coverage for time_field/mod.rs is unknown beyond the two explicitly addressed branches.
The snapshot-reviewed label reapplication after each snap-touching push is a manual step that could be automated via a CI hook or post-push script.
